---
role: proof
locale: en
of_concept: proposition-7.14-universal-property-blowup
source_book: gtm052
source_chapter: "II"
source_section: "7"
---

**Proof.** Since the question is local on $X$, we may assume $X = \operatorname{Spec} A$ is affine. Let $I \subseteq A$ be the ideal corresponding to $\mathcal{I}$, generated by elements $a_0, \ldots, a_n$. Then $\tilde{X}$ is the subscheme of $\mathbf{P}_A^n$ defined by the homogeneous ideal $\ker \varphi$, where $\varphi: A[x_0, \ldots, x_n] \to \bigoplus_{d \geqslant 0} I^d$ sends $x_i$ to $a_i$ in degree 1.

Now let $f: Z \to X$ be a morphism such that $f^{-1}\mathcal{I} \cdot \mathcal{O}_Z = \mathcal{L}$ is an invertible sheaf on $Z$. Set $s_i = f^{-1}(a_i) \in \Gamma(Z, \mathcal{L})$. By assumption, the $s_i$ generate $\mathcal{L}$ globally, since the $a_i$ generate $I$. By (7.1), the $s_i$ determine a morphism $g: Z \to \mathbf{P}_A^n$ with the property that $\mathcal{L} \cong g^*\mathcal{O}(1)$ and $s_i = g^{-1}x_i$ under this isomorphism.

I claim that $g$ factors through the closed subscheme $\tilde{X}$ of $\mathbf{P}_A^n$. This follows from the fact that if $F(x_0, \ldots, x_n)$ is a homogeneous element of degree $d$ in $\ker \varphi$, then $F(a_0, \ldots, a_n) = 0$ in $A$, and so $F(s_0, \ldots, s_n) = 0$ in $\Gamma(Z, \mathcal{L}^d)$. Thus $g(Z) \subseteq \tilde{X}$.

For uniqueness, any such morphism $g$ must satisfy $g^*\mathcal{O}_{\tilde{X}}(1) \cong f^{-1}\mathcal{I} \cdot \mathcal{O}_Z$, and the sections $s_i$ of $\mathcal{L}$ must be the pullbacks of the sections $x_i$ of $\mathcal{O}(1)$ on $\mathbf{P}_A^n$. Hence uniqueness follows from the uniqueness assertion of (7.1). $\square$
